geopolitical.market_impact: Oil at $91.95 is directly elevating inflation fears, pushing Treasury yields higher and weighing on rate-sensitive tech. The semis cluster is most exposed: higher energy costs pressure datacenter economics, while sanctions risk threatens China-exposed names like ASML and MU. The Singapore banks face a dual squeeze from rising US rates via DXY strength and MAS tightening.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Houthi attacks on Saudi tankers in the Red Sea added a second maritime chokepoint to the existing Hormuz blockade, spiking WTI 3.9%. Intel's night-earnings beat after the close was a positive counterbalance.

base.description: Stalemate continues — WTI hovers $90-95, US holds rates, no resolution. Semis consolidate; Singapore banks grind higher on earnings with no NIM relief.

optimistic.description: Ceasefire talks progress, Strait of Hormuz reopens partially — WTI drops to $75-80, easing inflation fears and allowing the Fed to hold. Tech rallies, banks benefit from rate stability.

pessimistic.description: Full Hormuz closure + Red Sea blockade persists — WTI spikes above $100, ECB and Fed forced to hike. Semis sell off sharply, Singapore banks hit by loan quality fears.

wildcard.description: China-US tariff rollback on $30B trade (per Reuters) accelerates, reducing supply chain friction. Tech outperforms, memory and photonics names surge on improved sentiment.
